question_text: In Australia (Southern Hemisphere), December is the summer season. Explain why the UK has winter at the same time, and calculate the total difference in day length between a UK winter day (8 hours of sunlight) and an Australian summer day (16 hours of sunlight) on the winter solstice.
correct_answer: Explanation: On 21 December, Earth's Southern Hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun (summer there) while the Northern Hemisphere is tilted away (winter in UK). The difference in day length = 16 hours - 8 hours = 8 hours longer in Australia.
explanation: This question combines two skills: (1) explaining why hemispheres have opposite seasons (tilt direction), and (2) a simple one-step subtraction to find the difference in day length. It reinforces that axial tilt is the cause and shows the quantitative effect on hours of daylight.
